title: Admin-Editable Contact Us, Customer Project Gallery, and Admin Icon Login

requirements.summary: Wire ContactInfoManagementPage into admin dashboard as a quick-link card labeled 'Edit Contact Info'
acceptanceCriteria: Admin dashboard has a visible 'Edit Contact Info' quick-link card
Clicking the card navigates to ContactInfoManagementPage
The page is protected by AdminGuard and only accessible to logged-in admins
Admin can update email, phone, address, and Google Maps link and save changes
Updated contact info is immediately reflected on the public ContactUsPage

file_operations.description: Add a new quick-link card with label 'Edit Contact Info' (English) and 'సంప్రదింపు సమాచారాన్ని సవరించండి' (Telugu) that navigates to '/admin/contact-info'. Position it alongside existing quick-link cards (Manage Quotations, Manage Users, Delivery Pricing). Use the Mail or Contact icon from lucide-react. The card should be visible to all authenticated admins and use the same styling as existing dashboard cards.

Verify that the route '/admin/contact-info' for ContactInfoManagementPage is already registered and wrapped with AdminGuard. If not present, add the route with lazy import: { path: '/admin/contact-info', lazy: () => import('./pages/admin/ContactInfoManagementPage') } and ensure AdminGuard wrapper is applied via the route configuration.

requirements.summary: Remove authentication gates from ProjectGalleryPage to make it fully public while preserving admin-only controls
acceptanceCriteria: ProjectGalleryPage is accessible to unauthenticated (guest) users without any login prompt
Each project card displays the project image, title, description, and category
Clicking a project card opens a detail view or expanded view with full project information
Category filter buttons are visible and functional for all users
Add/Edit/Delete project controls are only shown to authenticated admin users

file_operations.description: Remove any conditional authentication checks or login prompts that block unauthenticated users from viewing the gallery. Ensure useGetAllProjects hook is called unconditionally (not guarded by authentication state). Keep the category filter buttons (All, Banner, Digital, Offset, Design) visible and functional for all users. Ensure the 'Upload New Project' button and any edit/delete controls remain conditionally rendered based on isAdmin check from useIsAdmin hook. Verify each project card displays imageUrl, title, description, and category for all visitors.

Verify the route '/gallery' for ProjectGalleryPage is registered without any authentication or AdminGuard wrapper. The route should be publicly accessible. If the route has an auth guard, remove it. Ensure lazy loading: { path: '/gallery', lazy: () => import('./pages/ProjectGalleryPage') }.

requirements.summary: Add a project detail modal to ProjectGalleryPage that opens when a user clicks a project card
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking any project card opens a detail dialog/modal
The modal shows the full-size project image, project title, full description, and category
The modal has a close button and can be dismissed by clicking outside or pressing Escape
The detail view is accessible to all users including unauthenticated guests

file_operations.description: Create a new modal component that accepts props: open (boolean), onClose (callback), and project (Project type from backend.d.ts). Use shadcn/ui Dialog component for the modal structure with Dialog, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogDescription. Render the project.imageUrl as a large image using tag with object-fit cover. Display project.title in DialogTitle, project.description in DialogDescription, and project.category as a badge or label below the description. Include a close button (X icon) in the DialogHeader. Ensure the modal is dismissible by clicking the backdrop or pressing Escape (Dialog default behavior). The modal should be fully accessible to unauthenticated users. Add bilingual support for the close button using useLanguage context.

Import and integrate ProjectDetailModal component. Add local state to track the selected project (useState<Project | null>). When a project card is clicked, set the selected project in state. Render ProjectDetailModal with open={!!selectedProject}, onClose={() => setSelectedProject(null)}, and project={selectedProject}. Ensure clicking any project card (including from filtered results) opens the modal with full project details. The modal must be accessible to all users (no authentication check).

requirements.summary: Add an admin icon to the app header that navigates to login for guests or dashboard for authenticated admins
acceptanceCriteria: An admin icon (shield or settings icon) is visible in the header or navigation area
Clicking the icon when not logged in redirects to /admin/login
Clicking the icon when logged in as admin redirects to /admin/dashboard
The icon is always visible but does not expose admin controls to non-admins
After admin login, clicking the same icon goes directly to the admin dashboard without showing the login page again

file_operations.description: Add an admin icon button to the header section (next to LanguageToggle and LoginButton). Use ShieldCheck or Settings icon from lucide-react. Import useInternetIdentity hook to check authentication state (identity). Import useIsAdmin hook to check if the authenticated user is an admin. Import useNavigate from TanStack Router. On click: if not authenticated (!identity), navigate to '/admin/login'; if authenticated and isAdmin, navigate to '/admin/dashboard'; if authenticated but not admin, navigate to '/admin/login' (they may need to authenticate as admin). The icon should be visible to all users (always rendered) with a neutral color (e.g., text-gray-600 hover:text-gray-900). Add aria-label for accessibility: 'Admin Access'.
